lines.01: Commissions won't end immediately upon leaving a Hollow when you Pause. You can log in to the HDD system at the workshop to resume them.
lines.02: Resonia are a unique by-product of the Hollows. They contain Ether energy that can aid in your Hollow exploration. However, they are highly unstable and cannot be taken out of the Hollows.
lines.03: Gear Coins are a special currency exclusively used in the Hollows. You can use Gear Coins to trade with Bangboo merchants for various items and supplies.
lines.04: Bangboo merchants are robot traders traveling through the Hollows. These agile explorers carry a diverse selection of supplies and valuable items. Gear Coins are the only things they care for, so make sure to collect as many as possible if you want to trade with them!
lines.05: Pay attention to the areas marked with a magnifier icon on your screen. You can gather more map info in these locations to unlock paths that were previously inaccessible to you.
lines.06: You'll suffer a random Corruption when the Pressure of your squad reaches 100. Go to the HDD interface to view your Corruptions in the bottom-left corner.
lines.07: The events you encounter during Hollow exploration will increase your Pressure.
lines.60: Inside the Miasmic Field, the Miasma barrier transforms into a Miasmic Shield and enhances Boss enemies. Use Disintegration to break the Miasmic Shield's weak points and weaken it.
lines.61: Interacting with an Observation Singularity in the scene will mark the locations of rare supplies within the area on your map.
lines.62: The Fissure Beacon enables quick travel between areas and fully restores HP, effectively healing badly injured Agents.
lines.63: Supply piles in the Hollow can only be collected once and will not respawn.
lines.64: Small supply collection points in the Hollow can be accessed multiple times and will respawn the next day, but note that there's a weekly limit on total collections.
lines.65: Agents exposed to Miasma will suffer from Miasma Contamination. When it maxes, the whole squad will lose HP.
lines.66: Miasma Contamination will gradually fade when moving away from the toxic fog. Getting close to a Miasma Purifier can also quickly remove the contamination status.
lines.67: Some enemy skills will generate a Miasma Pool on the ground, which causes additional effects on certain enemy moves.
lines.68: When encountering an unstable structure in the scene, you can use Revelation on it to reveal hidden objects.
lines.69: When you encounter a Clue in the scene, you can use Insight to generate a guiding line leading to the next one.
lines.70: Some Bosses will activate the Miasmic Field. Enemies within gain massive DEF and damage reduction bonuses, as well as increased Anti-Interrupt capabilities.
lines.71: Investigation Zone Explore and interact in a vast open space - with careful investigation, you'll discover unexpected delights in every hidden corner.

lines.08: The trending theme at the video store changes daily. Stocking videotapes according to customer preference brings higher income. Sometimes, special guests also come to visit.
lines.09: Bardic Needle is a music store run by Elfy. When visited by Proxies, the store additionally provides Drive Disc services.
lines.10: Waterfall Soup is a popular noodle shop run by General Chop. Have a taste of General Chop's culinary art before venturing into a Hollow for a significant performance improvement.
lines.11: Use the Hollow Deep Dive (HDD) System to select the Hollow you want to enter and start a commission.
lines.12: The Godfinger arcade run by Asha is the go-to place for awesome minigames. Gather your friends on a nice day and see who comes out on top!
lines.13: The 141 Convenience Store is staffed by three adorable Bangboo clerks. Despite their constant chatter, they are very attentive, and the convenience store boasts an extensive variety of products. Give it some time and you'll surely discover great finds.
lines.14: Visit the gadget store run by Susie, Box Galaxy, as a Proxy to access W-Engine services.
lines.15: Enzo's remodeling shop, Turbo, is a specialized shop for automobile and motorbike remodeling. When you visit as a Proxy, he offers Bangboo remodeling services as well.
lines.16: Use Random Play's business car to reach distant areas. The FOORKS minivan is everyone's favorite. Economical and great for transporting cargo — a reliable choice that offers excellent value for your money.
lines.17: Press the Inter-Knot button in the Quick Menu to view featured posts and your schedule, as well as claim your commission payments.
lines.18: Press the DM button in the Quick Menu to view your text messages and group chats, accept commissions, and move them to your schedule.
lines.19: Press the Travel button in the Quick Menu to quickly navigate to the desired location.
lines.20: Press the Agent button in the Quick Menu to view Agent profiles, upgrade your Agents, and modify their equipment.
lines.21: Press the Storage button in the Quick Menu to check the supplies you own.
lines.22: Press Start Tracking in the Schedule interface to track your current commission.
lines.23: Talk to the people on the street! Perhaps you'll run into some delightful surprises!
lines.24: You can talk to Wise or Belle to switch your character during Free Roaming. There is more than one manager at Random Play!
lines.25: Free Roaming is a great opportunity for you to increase your Inter-Knot level, explore the city, and bond with your Agents.
lines.59: You can turn on Mission Focus in the Marker Selection on the map screen to only display icons for currently tracked missions.

lines.26: During their EX Special Attacks, Agents get a powerful DMG Reduction buff, dealing massive DMG while being invulnerable.
lines.27: Besides Quick Assist, you can also use Defensive Assist and Evasive Assist to help on-field Agents in combat. Quick Assist, Defensive Assist, Evasive Assist, and Assist Follow-Up are collectively known as Assist Attacks.
lines.28: There are currently five known damage attributes: Ice, Fire, Electric, Physical, and Ether. Dealing damage of different attributes can counter different types of enemies.
lines.29: Once affected with Attribute Anomaly, enemies gain a certain level of resistance, reducing their likelihood of entering the state again.
lines.30: Some enemies are more vulnerable to certain damage attributes. Inflicting the corresponding type of Attribute Anomaly will trigger additional effects.
lines.31: When an enemy has a low resistance to a particular attribute, the DMG numbers for that attribute will flash white, and a golden triangle will appear on the attribute icon to the right of its HP.
lines.32: Characters accumulate Decibels individually, allowing you to decide when to use whose Ultimate.
lines.33: Hitting Stunned enemies with heavy attacks can trigger Chain Attacks. The number of Chain Attacks is affected by the strength of the enemy and the number of available Agents in your squad.
lines.34: Some enemies have breakable parts. When parts are broken, certain enemies may alter their attacks or forms.
lines.35: Dodge can be used twice in quick succession. After that, it will enter a short cooldown.
lines.36: Stunned enemies are unable to move and sustain additional damage.
lines.37: When switching characters while launching an attack, the character switching out will not leave the field until the last action is completed.
lines.38: You can trigger a Perfect Assist whenever a golden prompt appears prior to enemy attacks. When a red prompt appears, it means only a Perfect Dodge may be performed.
lines.51: Using Dodge Counter, Defensive Assist, or Ultimate to diminish the Miasmic Shield more quickly.
lines.52: When [Miasma Contamination] reaches its maximum value, it deals DMG equal to a percentage of the current HP to all Agents in the team.
lines.53: When enemies have Miasmic Shields, they cannot be Stunned and gain massive DEF and damage reduction bonuses.
lines.54: When the Miasmic Shield is broken, enemies will take Purify DMG and recover Energy or Adrenaline for the Agent.
lines.55: Agents with the Rupture specialty possess great offense and survival capabilities, excelling at using Sheer DMG that ignores enemy defense to deal massive damage.
lines.56: Sheer DMG is a type of DMG that Agents with the Rupture specialty can deal, ignoring enemy DEF and dealing direct DMG.
lines.57: Sheer Force increases Sheer DMG, and Rupture-specialized Agents have a portion of their ATK converted to Sheer Force.
lines.58: Adrenaline Generation Rate affects the Adrenaline gained through Automatic Adrenaline Accumulation, hitting enemies, etc.

lines.39: Welcome to New Eridu, a thriving metropolis where both opportunities and risks abound. Wanna make a name here? Courage to take on Hollow adventures is an absolute necessity!
lines.40: What are Bangboo? Multi-functional housework helpers? Not quite. Special robots designed for in-Hollow exploration? Hey, you're getting close. Lively, adorable, cuddly rabbit-like mascots? Bingo!
lines.41: Hollows — these ever-expanding spherical realms appear out of thin air, as a manifestation of calamity. Overflowing with lethal Ether, they devour and corrupt everything unlucky enough to find itself within them.
lines.42: The Hollow Investigative Association (HIA) is the official organization approved by New Eridu's authorities for Hollow operations. Its primary responsibilities include recruiting, training, and managing Hollow Investigators.
lines.43: Although Sixth Street may be modest in size, it still continues to capture the hearts of the city's residents.
lines.44: Miasma Origins: An Ether derivative unique to the Lemnian Hollow.
lines.45: Miasma Properties: Miasma particles have potent hallucinogenic effects.
lines.46: Miasma Derivatives: Miasma evolves into more dangerous structures.
lines.47: Failume Heights is the main residential area of Waifei Peninsula.
lines.48: Waifei Peninsula is a major resource and processing center.
lines.49: Waifei name originates from guarding anomalies.
lines.50: Zenith Aerospace City is now mostly lost to the Hollow.
